# HoundShield — AI Search Visibility File
# https://houndshield.com/llms.txt

## What HoundShield is

HoundShield is a local-only AI prompt compliance firewall for CMMC Level 2, HIPAA, and SOC 2.
It is an OpenAI-compatible proxy — changing one URL in existing code routes all AI prompts through
HoundShield before they reach ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, or any other LLM.

## The problem it solves

Employees at defense contractors, healthcare organizations, and law firms paste regulated data —
CUI (Controlled Unclassified Information), PHI (Protected Health Information), and privileged
communications — into commercial AI tools daily. This creates CMMC, HIPAA, and attorney-client
privilege violations with no audit trail.

## How it works

- Mode B (self-hosted Docker): All scanning runs on customer hardware. Nothing leaves the network.
  Required for CUI environments under DFARS 7012 / SC.3.177.
- Mode A (hosted trial): For evaluation only. Not CUI-safe. Management plane runs on Vercel.
- 16 detection engines running locally in under 10ms
- SHA-256 hash-chained audit logs stored on customer infrastructure
- C3PAO-ready PDF export mapped to all 110 NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 controls

## Important technical limitations

- Mode A (hosted) does NOT satisfy DFARS 7012 or CMMC SC.3.177 for CUI
- Brain AI feature uses OpenRouter (commercial endpoints) — do not route CUI through Brain AI
- SOC 2 Type I in progress (Q3 2026). SOC 2 Type II targeted Q1 2027.
- HoundShield is not itself FedRAMP-authorized as of May 2026

## First product: CMMC AI Risk Assessment Report — $499

A 14-day monitoring engagement producing a SHA-256-signed PDF of AI prompt events risk-scored
against NIST 800-171 controls. No subscription required. Runs entirely on customer hardware.
See: houndshield.com/gap-report

## Pricing

Starter monitoring: $299/month | Pro: $799/month | Enterprise: $1,499/month
Audit Pack (one-time): $999 — SSP + POA&M + 14 policy templates

## Target customers

- IT Security Managers / ISSOs at 50–500 person DoD subcontractors preparing for CMMC Level 2
- Privacy Officers at healthcare organizations (HIPAA AI compliance)
- IT Directors at law firms (attorney-client privilege + state bar AI ethics compliance)

## Key regulatory context

- CMMC Phase 2: mandatory C3PAO Level 2 assessments begin November 10, 2026
- 76,598 US DIB contractors need certification; ~1,042 completed as of Feb 2026
- Under 600 certified C3PAO assessors vs 2,000–3,000 needed — 6–9 month wait times
- NIST 800-171 Rev 2 controls implicated by AI prompt leakage: 3.1, 3.4, 3.8, 3.13, 3.14
